designResults.topic: Huly.io hero animation teardown + implementation-ready rebuild spec for OURO PRAXIS generative network hero
designResults.summary: The famous Huly.io hero is NOT a live WebGL particle simulation. Direct inspection of huly.io's DOM and assets shows the heavy organic-network animation is a PRE-RENDERED looping video (hero.mp4 / hero.webm, dated 2024-06-07) sitting on a near-black background, with a static hero-illustration.jpg (2048x1138) as poster/fallback. The interactivity everyone remembers is a cheap, buttery cursor-following SPOTLIGHT reveal built in pure CSS: JS updates three CSS variables (--hero-mask-x, --hero-mask-y, --hero-mask-size) on pointer move, and two stacked copies of the illustration are revealed through a radial-gradient mask-image plus a clip-path:circle() with mix-blend-overlay, so a soft glowing brighter version of the art tracks the cursor. Built in Next.js + Tailwind by agency Pixel Point (who use Rive for the separate feature-section canvas animations, not the hero). The strategic lesson: bake the expensive generative network once as a seamless video, then layer a lightweight CSS spotlight for interactivity. For OURO PRAXIS I also spec a true live generative network (canvas 2D or three.js) since the brief asks for a generative math-driven hero, plus the exact palette and equations.

findings.point: The hero's heavy animation is a pre-rendered VIDEO, not a live sim
findings.detail: The <section class="hero"> contains a <video> with two sources: /videos/pages/home/hero/hero.mp4 and hero.webm (both ?updated=20240607). A static hero-illustration.7100a376.jpg at 2048x1138 is the poster/fallback and is also reused for the interactive overlay layers. So the flowing glowing network is baked pixels, streamed as a loop, giving perfect visual quality at ~zero runtime CPU/GPU cost.
findings.source: huly.io homepage DOM + /_next/static/media asset URLs (direct curl)

findings.point: Interactivity = a cursor-following radial 'spotlight/torch' reveal in pure CSS
findings.detail: Three CSS custom properties drive everything: --hero-mask-x, --hero-mask-y, --hero-mask-size (inline style on the hero image wrapper defaults --hero-mask-x:0px;--hero-mask-y:0px). A JS pointermove handler updates x/y (and animates size 0->full on load). Two stacked illustration layers: (1) base layer masked with [mask-image:radial-gradient(var(--hero-mask-size) at var(--hero-mask-x) var(--hero-mask-y), black 20%, transparent)]; (2) an overlay layer at z-10 with the SAME radial mask PLUS mix-blend-overlay and [clip-path:circle(var(--hero-mask-size) at var(--hero-mask-x) var(--hero-mask-y))]. Net effect: a soft-edged circular patch of a brighter/more-saturated version of the art follows the cursor and blends over the darker base — the 'reveal the light under your torch' feel.
findings.source: huly.io hero markup: mask-image / clip-path / mix-blend-overlay utility classes

findings.point: Color treatment is a near-black canvas with cool luminous highlights + one warm accent
findings.detail: Background colors in the CSS: #090A0C, #0b0d10, #0B0C0F, #111111, #000000 (Tailwind bg-grey-1). Headline uses a gradient clip: from-white via #d5d8f6 (lavender) to #fdf7fe (near-white pink). The single warm accent lives on the CTA glow: radial gradients of #FFFFF5 / #FFAA81 / #FFDA9F / rgba(210,106,58) — a soft ember. The network video itself reads as white/blue/violet glowing nodes and streaks on black with bloom.
findings.source: huly.io inline Tailwind classes and gradient definitions

findings.point: Stack + authorship
findings.detail: Next.js app (chunks under /_next/static/chunks, _next/image optimizer, Tailwind utility classes, Inter/font-title). Built by agency Pixel Point. The 5 canvas references on the page are product-feature sections (Rive-powered), NOT the hero; the hero uses zero canvas.
findings.source: Pixel Point case study (pixelpoint.io/case-studies/huly) + huly.io chunk/asset paths

findings.point: The mask has a soft feathered edge, not a hard circle
findings.detail: The radial-gradient stop is (black 20%, transparent) meaning the inner 20% of the mask radius is fully opaque and it feathers to transparent by the outer edge — a soft vignette spotlight. The separate clip-path:circle() gives the mix-blend-overlay layer a crisp containment while the gradient mask does the feathering. Replicate both for the exact look.

findings.point: No public source/teardown exists; recreations are generic
findings.detail: Searches for 'huly hero webgl teardown', github/codepen recreations returned only generic three.js particle-network tutorials (Codrops interactive particles, VoXelo codepen) and design-showcase listings (Saaspo, a-fresh). Nobody has published the actual technique — the DOM inspection above is the authoritative finding.

deliverables: EXACT HULY MECHANISM TO CLONE (Path A, recommended, most performant): (1) Render the ouroboros network as a seamless ~15-30s loop, export hero.webm (VP9) + hero.mp4 (H.264) + a poster.jpg fallback. (2) autoplay muted loop playsinline, object-fit:cover, on a #0A0B10 section. (3) Cursor spotlight: keep three CSS vars --mask-x,--mask-y,--mask-size on a wrapper; on pointermove set target px; each rAF frame lerp: x += (tx-x)*0.08. Two stacked copies of a BRIGHTER art layer: base uses mask-image:radial-gradient(var(--mask-size) at var(--mask-x) var(--mask-y), black 20%, transparent); overlay adds mix-blend-overlay + clip-path:circle(var(--mask-size) at var(--mask-x) var(--mask-y)). Intro: animate --mask-size 0px -> ~520px over ~1.2s ease-out on load.

LIVE GENERATIVE NETWORK (Path B, true math-driven, if you want no video): Canvas 2D is enough and dependency-free. Node count N = clamp(round(W*H/12000), 60, 220) (density 1 node / 12000 px^2). Each node {x,y, z in [0,1], vx,vy, r}. Depth: drawRadius = r*(0.6+0.9*z); alpha = 0.35+0.65*z (near = bigger/brighter). Drift: |v| ~ 0.15-0.3 px/frame; optional simplex-noise curl: angle = noise(x*0.001, y*0.001, t*0.0002)*TAU, v += 0.02*(cos,sin). Wrap at edges.

PROXIMITY LINES equation: for each pair with d = hypot(dx,dy) < D (D=150px), lineAlpha = pow(1 - d/D, 2) * 0.5 * min(zi,zj); stroke additive (globalCompositeOperation='lighter'). Use a spatial hash grid (cell=D) to keep it O(n) instead of O(n^2) above ~250 nodes.

ENERGY-FLOW STREAKS (the 'living' signature): maintain a pool of pulses. Every ~250ms spawn 1-2 pulses on random existing edges: pulse={a,b,t:0,speed:0.008-0.02}. Each frame t+=speed; draw a bright comet at P = lerp(A,B,t) with a short fading tail (3-5 samples behind t), core color #FFFFFF, additive, radius 2-3px + shadowBlur 8. Remove at t>1. This produces the flowing light travelling through the network.

CURSOR INTERACTION: lerp a virtual cursor (m.x += (targetX-m.x)*0.08). Two effects layered: (a) parallax — translate whole field by (m.x/W-0.5)*24*z and (m.y/H-0.5)*24*z so near nodes move more; (b) spotlight reveal (matches Huly) — within radius R=220px of cursor multiply node/line alpha by 1+1.2*(1-dist/R) and boost pulse spawn near cursor. Optional gentle attraction: nodes in R get v += (cursor-node)*0.0006.

PALETTE (OURO PRAXIS pastel-premium, dark hero): background radial/vertical gradient #0A0B10 -> #0E1018 (or #10111A center glow). Nodes/lines cool set: core #FDF7FE, lavender #D5D8F6, periwinkle #A9B4F5. Energy pulses: bright #FFFFFF with slight #CBD3FF bloom. Rebirth/warm accent pulses (sparse, ~1 in 6, for the death->rebirth theme): #FFDA9F -> #FFAA81 ember, echoing Huly's CTA glow #FFAA81/#FFDA9F/rgba(210,106,58). Headline gradient text: linear-gradient(135deg, #FFFFFF 30%, #D5D8F6 80%, #FDF7FE).

BLOOM/GLOW: Canvas 2D — fake bloom via shadowBlur (8-16) on additive dots + one half-res offscreen pass blurred and drawn back with 'lighter' at ~0.4 alpha. three.js path — Points(BufferGeometry) for nodes, LineSegments rebuilt per frame (or a line shader) for connections, EffectComposer + UnrealBloomPass (strength ~0.8, radius 0.4, threshold 0.85), additive PointsMaterial with a soft circular sprite texture. Use three.js only if you need 1000+ nodes / true 3D depth; otherwise canvas 2D looks identical at this scale.

OUROBOROS MOTIF INTEGRATION: bias node spawning along a large faint circle/torus path (r ~ 38% of min(W,H), jitter +-8%) so the network subtly reads as a ring devouring itself; run one persistent bright pulse continuously around the ring clockwise (the serpent) while random pulses fire across chords. This ties the generative field to the brand without a literal drawn snake.

PERFORMANCE + A11Y: DPR-aware canvas (cap devicePixelRatio at 2); pause rAF via IntersectionObserver when hero scrolls out and via visibilitychange when tab hidden; on mobile drop to N<=70 or swap to the static poster + baked video; honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce -> freeze to a single rendered still frame (no motion). Single rAF loop, delta-time based so speed is framerate-independent.

STACK RECOMMENDATION: Ship Path A (baked video + CSS spotlight) for the marketing hero — it is exactly what Huly does, is the 'retardmaxxed' choice (perfect visuals, ~0 runtime cost, trivial to make buttery), and lets an artist author the ouroboros in Blender/After Effects/Rive. Keep Path B (canvas 2D generative) as the interactive fallback / for a section where genuine live responsiveness matters. Do NOT reach for three.js unless a specific shot needs real 3D — at 60-220 nodes plain Canvas 2D with additive blending + shadowBlur is indistinguishable and has zero dependencies.

designResults.topic: Math for a Huly-grade generative ouroboros hero animation (pastel, light bg) — technique comparison + recommended hybrid with exact equations
designResults.summary: Five candidate techniques for a premium generative hero, each with its governing equations and tradeoffs, then a concrete recommended hybrid: a slowly-rotating parametric ouroboros ring (lemniscate/superellipse base curve) seeded with ~2,500 particles that are advected by divergence-free curl noise while spring-bound to the ring, overlaid with a spatial-hash proximity-line graph and a Gaussian "birth pulse" traveling around the loop from the mouth. All parametric equations, the curl-of-fBm-potential flow field, semi-implicit particle integrator, pulse phase function, cursor-parallax math, pastel palette, and performance budget (DPR cap, spatial hash, RAF dt-clamp, offscreen/WebGL path) are specified concretely enough to implement in vanilla Canvas2D or WebGL. Recommendation: vanilla Canvas2D + simplex-noise at ~2,500 particles is the aesthetic/perf sweet spot for the pastel-lines look; escalate to three.js/regl GPU points only for a 3D torus-knot variant at 20k+ particles.

findings.point: (a) Particle network with proximity-line graph
findings.detail: Positions p_i wander (random walk or eased drift); for every pair with dist_ij < d_max draw a line at alpha a_ij = a_max*(1 - dist_ij/d_max)^2 (quadratic falloff reads softer than linear). The look is the classic 'constellation' hero. Cost is dominated by line OVERDRAW, not particle count; naive pair test is O(n^2) and dies past ~800 particles. MUST use a uniform spatial-hash grid (cell size = d_max, test only the 3x3 neighbor cells) to get O(n*k), and cap connections/particle (~4) to bound fill. Tradeoff: gorgeous but generic/overused; on its own it lacks a narrative motif. Keep it only as an OVERLAY on a stronger structure.

findings.point: (b) Curl-noise / flow-field particle advection
findings.detail: The Huly.io signature motion. Build a scalar potential psi(x,y,t) from Perlin/simplex fBm, then advect particles by its 2D curl v = (dpsi/dy, -dpsi/dx). Because v is the curl of a potential it is divergence-free (incompressible): particles swirl in eddies and NEVER clump into sinks — that is exactly why it looks alive rather than converging. Finite-difference the potential (eps ~ 1px). fBm potential gives multi-scale eddies. Tradeoff: mesmerizing organic flow but shapeless on its own — needs a boundary/attractor to give it a silhouette (here, the ring).
findings.source: Bridson et al. 'Curl-Noise for Procedural Fluid Flow' (2007)

findings.point: (c) Parametric closed-loop curves (superellipse / torus knot / lemniscate)
findings.detail: Gives an EXACT ouroboros silhouette. Options: circle C(t)=(R cos t, R sin t); superellipse (Lame) for the premium soft-square ring, x=A*sgn(cos t)|cos t|^(2/n), y=B*sgn(sin t)|sin t|^(2/n) with n~2.5-4; torus knot (p,q coprime) x=(2+cos qt)cos pt, y=(2+cos qt)sin pt, z=sin qt for a 3D braided serpent; Bernoulli lemniscate (figure-8) x=A cos t/(1+sin^2 t), y=A sin t cos t/(1+sin^2 t) whose SELF-CROSSING is thematically the mouth-meets-tail. Tradeoff: precise and on-brand but static/cold if rendered as a bare line — it is the skeleton, not the flesh. Use it as the seeding manifold for particles.
findings.source: standard analytic geometry (Lame curves, torus knots, lemniscate of Bernoulli)

findings.point: (d) De Jong / Clifford strange attractors
findings.detail: Iterated maps producing dense filamentary ink-cloud forms. Clifford: x'=sin(a*y)+c*cos(a*x), y'=sin(b*x)+d*cos(b*y). De Jong: x'=sin(a*y)-cos(b*x), y'=sin(c*x)-cos(d*y). Millions of accumulated point-hits build a self-similar texture; animate by slowly drifting a,b,c,d. Tradeoff: hypnotic and unique BUT chaotic/uncontrollable — you cannot force it into an ouroboros ring, and it wants a dark additive canvas to glow. Wrong fit for a light pastel, on-motif hero; mention as an alternative 'texture-fill' inside the ring band at most.
findings.source: Peter de Jong / Clifford Pickover attractor maps

findings.point: (e) Metaballs / gradient mesh
findings.detail: Implicit field f(x,y)=sum_i r_i^2 / ((x-x_i)^2+(y-y_i)^2); threshold f>=T for organic blobs that merge/split (marching squares on CPU, or a fragment shader on GPU as the far cheaper path). Gradient-mesh = animated multi-stop conic/linear gradients (or a low-poly Delaunay mesh with drifting vertex colors) for a soft aurora wash. Tradeoff: superb premium BACKGROUND ambience and great for a 'living' pastel gradient behind the ring, but it is diffuse — no crisp particle detail or line graph, so it cannot carry the hero alone. Use as the backdrop layer.
findings.source: Blinn metaballs / marching squares; conic-gradient mesh technique

findings.point: RECOMMENDED HYBRID (layered)
findings.detail: Layer 0: soft metaball/gradient-mesh wash (e) as pastel backdrop. Layer 1: the STRUCTURE = parametric loop (c) — lemniscate or superellipse ring — used purely as the manifold to seed ~2,500 particles in a Gaussian-thickness band. Layer 2: MOTION = each particle advances in ring-phase (circulation omega) while curl noise (b) perturbs it and a spring pulls it back to the ring, so the silhouette holds but breathes. Layer 3: proximity-line graph (a) via spatial hash for the constellation shimmer. Layer 4: a Gaussian BIRTH PULSE travels around the loop from the mouth (lemniscate crossing), boosting size/brightness and shifting hue dead->alive as it passes — the ouroboros rebirth beat. Cursor parallax tilts the ring and depth-shifts layers. This fuses the four strong techniques so each covers the others' weakness: shape from (c), life from (b), sparkle from (a), ambience from (e).

deliverables: RING BASE CURVE (choose one, ouroboros mouth at t=0). Superellipse (premium soft-square): x(t)=A*sign(cos t)*|cos t|^(2/n), y(t)=B*sign(sin t)*|sin t|^(2/n), n in [2.5,4], t in [0,2pi). Circle: C(t)=(R cos t, R sin t). Lemniscate (figure-8, self-crossing = mouth eats tail): x(t)=A*cos t/(1+sin^2 t), y(t)=A*sin t*cos t/(1+sin^2 t). Organic wobble: multiply radius by (1 + 0.06*sin(3t + 0.2*T_time)). Tangent T(t)=C'(t)/|C'(t)|; unit normal N(t)=rot90(T(t)) = (-T.y, T.x).

PARTICLE SEEDING on the ring band: particle i holds base phase theta_i uniform in [0,2pi) and radial offset rho_i ~ truncated Gaussian(mean 0, sigma = bandThickness ~ 0.06*R). Rest position: base_i = C(theta_i) + rho_i * N(theta_i). Assign depth d_i in [0,1] (for parallax) and a per-particle noise seed. Precompute a ring LUT: sample C(theta) at 512 evenly spaced thetas so you never call trig per-particle per-frame.

CURL-NOISE FLOW FIELD (divergence-free). Scalar fBm potential: psi(x,y,t) = sum_{o=0..O} (gain^o) * simplex3( x*freq*lac^o, y*freq*lac^o, t*tScale ), with O=3, gain=0.5, lac=2.0, freq ~ 0.0035 per px, tScale ~ 0.08. 2D curl by central difference (eps=1px): vx = (psi(x,y+eps) - psi(x,y-eps))/(2*eps); vy = -(psi(x+eps,y) - psi(x-eps,y))/(2*eps). This v is incompressible so particles swirl without collapsing to sinks — the core of the Huly feel.

PARTICLE INTEGRATOR (semi-implicit Euler, ring-bound + flow). Per frame with clamped dt: (1) advance phase: theta_i += omega*dt (omega ~ 0.06 rad/s for the slow rotation). (2) recompute rest target: target_i = C(theta_i) + rho_i*N(theta_i). (3) spring toward ring: F_spring = k*(target_i - p_i), k ~ 6.0. (4) flow perturbation: F_flow = A_flow * curl(p_i, T_time), A_flow ~ 22 px. (5) integrate: vel_i += dt*(F_spring + F_flow); vel_i *= damping (0.86); p_i += dt*vel_i. k controls how tightly particles hug the silhouette vs. how freely they smear into the flow — tune k/A_flow ratio for the 'liquid outline' look.

TRAVELING BIRTH PULSE (rebirth from the mouth). Pulse phase: phi(t) = (2*pi * (T_time / T_loop)) mod 2pi, T_loop ~ 7s. Angular distance of particle to pulse: dtheta = min(|theta_i - phi|, 2pi - |theta_i - phi|). Gaussian gain: g_i = exp( -(dtheta^2) / (2*w^2) ), pulse width w ~ 0.30 rad. Apply: size_i = s0*(1 + 1.8*g_i); alpha_i = base_a + 0.6*g_i; color_i = lerpHSL(deadColor, aliveColor, g_i). Birth the pulse at theta=0 (lemniscate crossing / the mouth) so the light is 'swallowed' at the tail and reborn — the ouroboros loop.

PROXIMITY-LINE GRAPH via spatial hash. Grid cell size = d_max (~28px). Each frame: clear grid, bucket particles by floor(p/cell). For each particle, test only the 3x3 neighboring cells; if dist_ij < d_max and connection count < 4, draw line alpha a_ij = a_max*(1 - dist_ij/d_max)^2 (a_max ~ 0.5). O(n*k) not O(n^2). On a LIGHT bg use ctx.globalCompositeOperation='multiply' (or 'source-over' at low alpha) so overlapping pastel lines deepen softly instead of blowing out; reserve 'lighter'/additive only for dark backdrops.

CURSOR PARALLAX MATH. Normalize mouse to m=(mx,my) in [-1,1] from viewport center. Smooth: m_s += (m - m_s)*0.08 each frame (critically-damped feel). Depth parallax per particle: p_draw_i = p_i + m_s * P_max * d_i (P_max ~ 18px; near particles d_i~1 move most). Whole-ring pseudo-3D tilt: rotate the field by angle = m_s.x*maxTilt (maxTilt ~ 0.05 rad) and fake pitch by scaling drawn y by cos(m_s.y*maxTilt). For the 3D torus-knot variant, instead map m_s to camera yaw/pitch.

PERFORMANCE BUDGET. Particle count: 2,000-3,000 for the Canvas2D lines look (line overdraw is the ceiling), 15k-40k for WebGL points-only. DPR: canvas.width = cssW * Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2); ctx.setTransform(dpr,0,0,dpr,0,0) — cap DPR at 2 (pastel low-contrast fill on retina is wasteful past 2x). RAF loop with dt clamp: dt = Math.min((now-last)/1000, 1/30) so a backgrounded tab does not teleport particles on refocus. Move physics to an OffscreenCanvas + Web Worker if main thread jank appears; transfer positions via a Float32Array. Bucket line draws by alpha into a few Path2D objects and stroke once per bucket to cut state changes. Pause RAF via IntersectionObserver when the hero scrolls out of view.

PASTEL PALETTE on soft background. BG: warm off-white #FBFAF7 or #F4F1EC (subtle radial vignette to #EFEAE2). Dormant/dead particles (base state): dusty lavender #C9C3D6, muted slate-blue #B8C4D0. Reborn/alive (pulse peak, via HSL lerp): pastel mint #B9E3D0, peach #F4C9B0, rose #F3C4D2, periwinkle #C3CBF0. Pulse core hue: soft gold #F0D9A8 -> coral #F2B8A2. Lines: dormant color at alpha 0.15-0.4. Keep saturation low (S ~ 25-45%) and lightness high (L ~ 75-88%) so it stays premium-pastel, not candy.

STACK RECOMMENDATION. Primary: vanilla Canvas2D + the 'simplex-noise' npm module, ~2,500 particles, spatial-hash lines — best aesthetic/effort ratio for the pastel-constellation-on-light look, ships in one self-contained file (good for an inline artifact under CSP). Escalate to three.js or regl with a GPU points shader (curl noise + integration in the vertex/fragment stage, instanced) only for the 3D torus-knot variant at 20k+ particles; there the proximity-line graph becomes the bottleneck (CPU/GPU sort), so drop lines or fake glow-halos in that mode. Keep the metaball/gradient-mesh backdrop as a cheap separate CSS conic-gradient or a low-res second canvas.

designResults.topic: Custom Ouroboros SVG logo + subtle background motif for OURO PRAXIS (parametric construction, golden-ratio proportions, self-drawing animation, giant watermark, 3 logo concepts)
designResults.summary: An ouroboros is a serpent-ring biting its own tail; the two hard problems are (1) tapering the body from a fat head to a thin tail and (2) the head-meets-tail "bite" join. SVG cannot taper a stroke natively, so a true taper must be built as a FILLED closed path whose outer and inner edges spread apart from tail to head; a hairline "single-line" mark instead uses a constant stroke with round caps and reads as premium/minimal. Golden-ratio proportions (hole radius = R/phi, max body width = 0.382R, golden-angle 137.5deg bite placement) keep it feeling designed, not clip-arty. The whole mark is best authored as a tiny parametric JS generator (params R, wTail, wHead, taperK, spiralOffset, headAngle) that emits a static `d` string you then ship inline. Self-drawing is done with pathLength=1 + stroke-dasharray:1 + animating stroke-dashoffset 1->0; the signature "eternal return" loop is a short bright dash traveling around the ring forever via a looping dashoffset. The giant watermark is the SAME path scaled 120-160% of viewport, opacity ~0.04, radial-gradient-masked so it dissolves, pointer-events:none, ultra-slow rotation. Three concepts: single continuous line ("The Single Breath"), geometric segmented scales ("The Segmented Cycle"), organic filled taper with sculpted biting head ("The Living Serpent"). Pastel low-saturation palette maps cold-corpse -> warm-life to reinforce death/rebirth.

findings.point: SVG has no native tapering stroke — a true variable-width serpent body must be a FILLED path, not a stroked one
findings.detail: stroke-width is a single scalar for the whole path; the long-standing proposal for per-segment widths (stroke-width='2,10,5') was never adopted. The practical technique (same one Inkscape's Power Stroke / Taper Stroke LPEs use) is to draw the body outline as a closed fill on an unstroked path: compute an OUTER edge and an INNER edge whose separation grows from tail to head. So the 'organic tapered serpent' concept is a fill, and the 'minimalist single-line' concept is a constant-width stroke — they are two different construction models, choose per concept.

findings.point: Self-drawing animation: set pathLength then animate stroke-dashoffset, and animate dasharray/offset (not just offset) for cross-browser consistency
findings.detail: Setting pathLength=1 normalizes the geometry so you can write stroke-dasharray:1; stroke-dashoffset:1 (fully hidden) and animate dashoffset to 0 to 'draw' it, regardless of the path's real length — no getTotalLength() JS needed. Browsers historically disagree on the offset direction, so for reliability animate the dash values and always test both directions. This works only on STROKED paths (the single-line and segmented concepts); a filled body cannot be dash-drawn and must instead be revealed with an animated clip-path/mask sweep or by self-drawing its two edge curves as strokes first then fading the fill in.

findings.point: A short bright dash looping around the ring = the ideal 'eternal return' brand motion, and it's cheap + accessible
findings.detail: Stack two strokes on the same path: a faint static base ring, plus a bright accent stroke with stroke-dasharray:'18 1000' animated by dashoffset from 0 to -1018 on infinite linear loop. This sends a travelling glint of light around the serpent forever (life-force circulating / death->rebirth) without WebGL. Pure CSS keyframes = 60fps, battery-friendly, and must be wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to freeze it.

findings.point: The head-meets-tail 'bite' is the make-or-break detail; solve it with a tiny spiral offset + sculpted head, not a hard crossing
findings.detail: Make the centerline a one-turn Archimedean spiral r(theta)=R + spiralOffset*(theta/2pi) with spiralOffset ~= wHead, so the head naturally passes just OUTSIDE the tail and overlaps it (real over/under depth) instead of colliding. Draw the head last/on-top, cut a small mouth notch (subtract the tail's cross-section circle), and add a background-colored sliver or soft shadow in the overlap to read as over/under. Minimalist versions can skip all this: a small gap + one eye-dot and the brain completes the 'biting'.
findings.source: synthesis of ouroboros construction + SVG path fill technique (schepers.cc/differentstrokes.html)

findings.point: Giant faint watermark should be the SAME vector reused and dissolved with a mask, never a separate low-res asset
findings.detail: Define the mark once as an SVG <symbol> and <use> it at 120-160% of the viewport, bleeding off an edge, opacity 0.03-0.06, pointer-events:none, z-index behind content. Feather it with a radial-gradient mask so edges melt into the pastel background (premium, not stamped), add ultra-slow rotation (>=120s) for a 'living' feel, and optional scroll parallax. Because it's vector it stays crisp at any scale; a hair of blur (filter: blur(0.5px)) softens aliasing on thin arcs.

deliverables: PARAMETRIC GENERATOR (author once, ship static d). Inputs: R (centerline radius), wTail, wHead (half-widths), taperK (~1.3), spiralOffset (~wHead), headAngle (mouth wedge span), N (samples 80-120). Loop theta 0->2pi: r(t)=R + spiralOffset*(t/2pi); w(t)=wTail + (wHead-wTail)*Math.pow(t/2pi, taperK); outer point = (cx+(r+w)*cos t, cy+(r+w)*sin t); inner point = (cx+(r-w)*cos t, cy+(r-w)*sin t). Build d by walking OUTER edge t:0->2pi as cubic-smoothed segments, round-cap the tail at t=0, sculpt the head wedge at t~2pi, then walk INNER edge t:2pi->0, then 'Z'. That single closed fill IS the tapered serpent (Concept 3).

GOLDEN-RATIO PROPORTION SHEET (phi=1.618). For outer radius R=100: inner hole radius = R/phi = 61.8; max body half-width wHead = R*(1-1/phi)/1 -> body thickness 0.382R = 38.2, so wHead=19.1, wTail=1.5 (near-point). Place the bite at the golden angle 137.5deg from vertical for a non-obvious, designed asymmetry. Eye sits at 1/phi along the head length. Head arc length = phi * (2*wHead). Canvas: square viewBox '0 0 200 200' with the mark occupying an inner circle of diameter 2R, i.e. mark:canvas ratio ~ 1/phi for breathing room.

CONCEPT 1 — 'THE SINGLE BREATH' (single continuous line). Constant hairline stroke, fill:none, stroke-linecap:round, stroke-linejoin:round, one open near-circular path whose ends overlap ~6deg; head = minimal 3-point wedge, one filled eye-dot. Ship as: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200'><path pathLength='1' d='...' fill='none' stroke='#6E5E7A' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/></svg>. Self-draw CSS: path{stroke-dasharray:1;stroke-dashoffset:1;animation:draw 2.4s ease forwards} @keyframes draw{to{stroke-dashoffset:0}} @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){path{stroke-dashoffset:0;animation:none}}. Best for favicon + wordmark lockup; most premium/minimal.

CONCEPT 2 — 'THE SEGMENTED CYCLE' (geometric/segmented). Define ONE tapered scale glyph (a lozenge/chevron) as <symbol id='scale'>; place M copies (M~34, a Fibonacci number) via <use> at theta_i = i*(2pi/M) with per-copy rotate(tangent) and scale s_i = lerp(0.25,1.0, i/M) so scales shrink tail->head. Gaps between scales = negative space = 'praxis/engineered' feel. Animate: stagger each <use> opacity/scale in around the ring (transition-delay: calc(i*30ms)) so the ring 'assembles'. Optional 2-tone: alternate #6E5E7A / #9AA0B0. All generated programmatically from the same theta sweep as the generator.

CONCEPT 3 — 'THE LIVING SERPENT' (organic filled taper). Use the parametric generator fill above with spiralOffset for a true over/under bite, sculpted open-mouth head biting the tail tip, golden-position eye, and a thin lighter inner-offset curve for dimensionality. Flat pastel fill (no outline) or soft gradient. Reveal animation (fills can't dash-draw): mask the fill with a conic-gradient and animate its angle 0->360deg (mask sweep), OR self-draw the two edge curves as strokes then fade fill in. Most illustrative — keep it flat/minimal to avoid clip-art.

SELF-DRAW + ETERNAL-RETURN LOOP (reusable). Draw: pathLength='1'; stroke-dasharray:1; animate stroke-dashoffset 1->0 (draw head-to-tail). Eternal loop (on top of base ring): <path class='glint' stroke-dasharray='18 1000'/> with @keyframes travel{to{stroke-dashoffset:-1018}} .glint{animation:travel 6s linear infinite}. Always guard with @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){*{animation:none!important}}.

GIANT WATERMARK RECIPE. Reuse the mark: <svg class='wm' aria-hidden='true'><use href='#ouro'/></svg>. CSS: .wm{position:fixed;inset:auto -20% -30% auto;width:160vmin;opacity:.045;color:#6E5E7A;pointer-events:none;z-index:-1;filter:blur(.4px);-webkit-mask:radial-gradient(closest-side, #000 55%, transparent 100%);mask:radial-gradient(closest-side,#000 55%,transparent 100%);animation:spin 180s linear infinite} @keyframes spin{to{transform:rotate(360deg)}}. Optionally cut the ouroboros OUT of a soft pastel gradient blob (use it as a <mask>) for a Huly-grade look.

PASTEL PREMIUM PALETTE (death->rebirth, all low-sat/high-value). Ink/serpent: #6E5E7A (dusty plum) / #4A3F57 (deep aubergine). Rebirth accent (the travelling glint): #E8C08A (soft gold) or #F4C7B5 (peach). Backgrounds: #F6F1EA (bone/cream), #ECE6F0 (misty lilac), #E6EDE6 (pale sage). Filled-body death->life gradient: #9AA0B0 (cold ash) -> #E9B7C4 (warm rose). Watermark = ink at 4-5% alpha. Keep saturation <40%, value >85% for the pastel register.

STACK RECOMMENDATION. Author geometry in a ~40-line ouroboros.ts generator returning a d string (deterministic, param-tweakable), iterate in a dev sandbox, then FREEZE the resolved <path d='...'> and ship it inline (zero runtime cost, cacheable, favicon-ready). Motion = SVG + CSS keyframes only (self-draw + travelling glint + watermark spin) — accessible, 60fps, no JS. Reserve canvas/WebGL ONLY if you later want a separate math-driven flow-field particle backdrop behind the hero; the ouroboros itself never needs it. Deliver an SVG <symbol> so logo, hero animation, and watermark all reference one source of truth.

designResults.topic: Premium pastel + minimalist web design system for OURO PRAXIS (death/rebirth, ouroboros, Awwwards-caliber)
designResults.summary: Premium pastel design is NOT bright candy color. It is a near-black warm "ink" on a warm off-white "paper" base, with pastels used sparingly as desaturated (30-45% saturation, 85-92% lightness) light-and-gradient accents — never as flat fills covering large areas. Four mechanics separate premium from childish: (1) desaturate and warm the pastels so they sit close to neutral; (2) ground everything on cream/off-white, never pure white; (3) add a fine film grain/noise overlay at 3-5% opacity to kill the flat "vector" cheapness; (4) enforce one dark or saturated anchor (deep plum ink here, fitting the ouroboros death theme) plus extreme negative space so the color reads as a whisper. Type should be an editorial serif display (light weight, tight tracking) paired with a neutral grotesque body, with a monospace for the revenue-calculator numerals. Below is a ready-to-build palette (hex), token set, type scale, spacing rhythm, motion spec, and 8 benchmark sites with what to steal from each.

findings.point: Pastels read premium only when desaturated AND warmed toward neutral
findings.detail: Stock pastels look childish because they keep high saturation at high lightness (HSL ~S70 L88). Premium pastels drop saturation to ~25-40% and nudge hue toward a warm/muted axis, so each color sits only a few steps off the cream base. The color should feel like tinted light, not paint. Target HSL band: S 25-42%, L 85-92%.

findings.point: Never ground on pure white — use warm off-white/cream
findings.detail: Pure #FFFFFF makes pastels look like a kids' app and blows out contrast. A warm paper base (#F7F4EE-ish) unifies every pastel, adds instant 'editorial/premium' feel, and lets near-black warm ink text carry the contrast. Neutral pastel palettes (ivory, pale peach, soft taupe) are described as the modern sophisticated route.

findings.point: One dark/saturated anchor prevents the 'washed-out' failure
findings.detail: The single most cited rule: if everything is light and airy, add one slightly saturated or dark anchor (navy/charcoal — here a deep aubergine plum) or the site looks washed out. For OURO the anchor doubles as narrative: the plum is the 'death/serpent' pole against the pastel 'rebirth' pastels.

findings.point: Fine grain/noise is the cheapness-killer
findings.detail: Adding gentle grain/texture over pastels is repeatedly recommended to give them depth and stop the flat vector look. Implementation: full-viewport fixed SVG feTurbulence overlay at 3-5% opacity, mix-blend-mode soft-light/overlay, pointer-events none. This one layer is what makes Family.co / Stripe-grade pastels feel expensive rather than templated.

findings.point: Editorial serif display + neutral grotesque body is the premium-minimal type formula
findings.detail: Award sites pair a large light-weight serif headline (authority, cultural weight, print feel) with a clean grotesque body (clarity). Keep display weight LIGHT (300-400) with tight negative tracking — bold pastel headlines read cheap. Free stack that matches paid look: Fraunces (variable, optical sizing, soft) for display + Geist or General Sans for UI/body.

findings.point: Pastels are proven in serious money/B2B contexts, not just lifestyle
findings.detail: Fintech dashboards, healthcare, and B2B sites use pastels intelligently; Mercury (banking) is the reference for soft peach/lavender washes reading trustworthy. This de-risks OURO PRAXIS selling revenue-reactivation to service businesses — pastel will not read unserious if grounded and anchored correctly.

findings.point: Whitespace is the primary premium signal, ahead of color
findings.detail: The strongest pastel sites are minimalist-first: ~85vh airy hero, one idea per section, generous section padding, short text measure. Color does less work than space. Benchmarks (Family, Linear, Vercel) deploy exactly one hero gradient moment and keep everything else ink-on-paper with hairline 1px borders at ~10% ink opacity rather than gray boxes.

deliverables: RECOMMENDED PALETTE (hex) — Base: --paper #F7F4EE (warm off-white canvas), --paper-raised #FCFAF5 (cards/surfaces), --paper-sunken #F0ECE3. Ink/text: --ink #171614 (warm near-black, primary text), --ink-soft #5C574E (secondary), --ink-faint #A39D92 (captions/borders base), hairline border = ink @ 10% = rgba(23,22,20,0.10). Pastels (desaturated, warmed): --lavender #DAD3F0, --blush #F1D6D3, --mint #CDE4D6, --peach #F6DDC7, --sky #CCDEEC, --cream #FBF3E4. Deeper tints for text-on-pastel / iconography: lavender-700 #7A6DA8, mint-700 #5E8C72, blush-700 #B07A74, sky-700 #5E7E98. ANCHOR (the 'death'/serpent pole, use for one CTA + deep sections + logo): --plum #3B2F4A (hover #2E2440); optional secondary anchor --clay #B5654A for warm rebirth accent. Use pastels ONLY as: hero mesh-gradient, section-tint backgrounds, small chips/tags, chart fills — never full-bleed saturated blocks.

HERO GRADIENT MESH — layer 3-4 pastels at low opacity as radial blobs over --paper, e.g. background: radial-gradient(60% 60% at 20% 20%, rgba(218,211,240,0.55), transparent 60%), radial-gradient(55% 55% at 85% 15%, rgba(246,221,199,0.45), transparent 60%), radial-gradient(60% 60% at 70% 90%, rgba(204,222,236,0.45), transparent 60%), var(--paper). Animate blob positions slowly (20-30s loop, ease-in-out) for the Huly/Stripe living-gradient effect.

GRAIN OVERLAY (mandatory cheapness-killer) — fixed full-viewport div, pointer-events:none, z above bg below content: background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml,...feTurbulence baseFrequency=0.8 numOctaves=4..."); opacity:0.04; mix-blend-mode:soft-light. Ship as a single inline SVG data-URI so it costs zero requests.

TYPE SYSTEM — Display: Fraunces (variable; opsz max, wght 300-400, soft/wonky axes on, tracking -0.02em, line-height 0.95-1.02) — the editorial serif carrying the death/rebirth gravitas. Body/UI: Geist Sans (or General Sans) wght 400, medium 500 for emphasis, line-height 1.6-1.65. Numerals/labels/calculator: Geist Mono (or Space Mono) — mono reinforces the 'cycle/counter' motif and makes the revenue calculator feel like an instrument. Load via next/font. Paid upgrade path if budget: PP Editorial New (display) + PP Neue Montreal (body).

TYPE SCALE (fluid, base 16px, ratio ~1.25 with an oversized display step) — Display/hero: clamp(3.5rem, 8vw, 8rem); H1: clamp(2.5rem, 5vw, 4rem); H2: clamp(2rem, 3.5vw, 3rem); H3: 1.5rem; body-lg: 1.25rem/1.6; body: 1.0625rem(17px)/1.65; small: 0.9375rem; label/eyebrow: 0.8125rem(13px) UPPERCASE, letter-spacing 0.10em, mono, --ink-soft. Text measure capped 60-68ch. Keep display weights light — no 700+ in pastel.

SPACING RHYTHM — 8px base grid; scale {4,8,12,16,24,32,48,64,96,128,192}. Section vertical padding clamp(6rem,12vw,12rem). Content max-width 1200-1280px; narrow prose column ~680px. Hero occupies ~85-90vh with heavy top air. Radii: cards/surfaces 20-24px, chips 999px pill, buttons 999px pill. SHADOWS: soft, large, low-opacity, and TINTED with the plum anchor (never pure black) — e.g. 0 24px 60px -24px rgba(59,47,74,0.18), 0 2px 6px rgba(59,47,74,0.06). Borders: 1px hairline rgba(23,22,20,0.10), not filled gray cards.

MOTION SPEC — Easing tokens: --ease-out expo cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1) for reveals; --ease-inout cubic-bezier(0.65,0,0.35,1) for loops. Durations: micro 180-260ms, entrance 600-900ms, hero draw 1200ms. Reveals: fade + 16-24px rise, staggered 60-80ms. OUROBOROS HERO: parametric ring drawn via SVG stroke-dashoffset OR an r3f/OGL particle ring (Huly-grade) forming a serpent-eating-tail circle; slow continuous rotation (~40s), particles cycling head->tail = the eternal return. Respect prefers-reduced-motion (freeze to a static drawn ring).

STACK RECOMMENDATION — Next.js (App Router) + Tailwind v4 (@theme tokens holding the hex vars above) + next/font (Fraunces + Geist + Geist Mono) + Framer Motion for scroll reveals + Lenis for smooth scroll + react-three-fiber/OGL (or a GLSL fragment shader) for the generative ouroboros hero + inline SVG feTurbulence grain. Deploy on Vercel. This mirrors the exact toolchain behind the benchmark sites and keeps the whole thing self-contained and fast.

BENCHMARK SITES (steal-this list) — (1) Family.co: THE pastel-on-cream benchmark; soft mesh gradients, colossal negative space, buttery motion — copy its restraint and gradient desaturation. (2) Huly.io: the generative/math hero benchmark (named in brief); study its particle motion craft for the ouroboros. (3) Linear.app: 'one hero gradient, everything else ink + hairlines' discipline; subtle pastel washes over dark. (4) Vercel/Geist (vercel.com): ink+paper+one-accent minimalism, perfect hairline borders and type scale. (5) Stripe.com: animated mesh-gradient hero — color as LIGHT not fill; the premium-gradient standard. (6) Arc / thebrowser.company (arc.net): playful pastels kept premium via craft + space — proof pastel can be bold yet upscale. (7) Mercury.com: pastel peach/lavender in serious fintech — de-risks pastel for OURO's money-focused B2B audience. (8) Rauno.me: micro-interaction and craft-minimalism reference for the small details. Sourcing galleries: godly.website and awwwards.com/websites/pastel.

designResults.topic: Tasteful high-end interaction patterns for the OURO PRAXIS landing page (death/rebirth ouroboros narrative), with reduced-motion fallbacks
designResults.summary: Premium in 2026 means surgical, meaningful motion, not "animate everything." The winning move for OURO PRAXIS is a single unifying mechanic: one continuous ouroboros ring SVG whose stroke is "drawn" by page scroll progress, so the whole site reads as one eternal loop that closes (serpent bites its own tail) exactly at the final CTA. Every other interaction (magnetic CTA, cursor-reactive field, animated counters, section "composting" transitions, the revenue calculator) is a small satellite of that spine and shares its easing vocabulary. Each pattern below has an explicit tasteful/slop line and a death-rebirth justification. Critically, all motion is layered ON TOP of a fully-functional finished static state and gated behind prefers-reduced-motion, so the reduced-motion experience is the real product (final states shown, values filled, calculator works) not a broken one. Ground all copy in the real numbers (41% reply vs 8% industry, GBP500k+ generated, 200k+ leads reactivated, 536% conversion lift, ~40-100k USD recovered/campaign). Recommended stack: Next.js + Lenis smooth-scroll + GSAP ScrollTrigger for the ring scrub, native CSS scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline: view()) for cheap section reveals, Motion/Framer Motion for the React calculator, hand-authored SVG ouroboros path. Keep it to ~6 interactions; more is slop.

findings.point: Restraint is THE premium signal in 2026
findings.detail: The 2022-23 'animate everything on scroll' era is over. Current high-converting/premium pages feel calmer: fewer competing elements, more breathing room, one or two tuned reveals per viewport, measured easing, minimal bounce. Motion should demonstrate meaning (show behavior) not decorate. For OURO PRAXIS: resist making every section a set-piece. The ouroboros ring carries the concept; everything else stays quiet. Slop = parallax on every block, springy bounces everywhere, scroll-jacking. Tasteful = the page still reads perfectly with JS off.

findings.point: Scroll-drawn ouroboros ring = the narrative spine (SVG stroke-dashoffset)
findings.detail: One continuous serpent path drawn as an SVG. Compute L = path.getTotalLength(); set stroke-dasharray:L and stroke-dashoffset:L (hidden). Bind offset to global scroll progress p in [0,1]: strokeDashoffset = L*(1-p). At the top the ring is unwritten; as you scroll the serpent's body draws around the viewport margin; at the CTA (p=1) the head meets the tail = the loop closes = dead leads reborn as revenue. Native path: animation-timeline: scroll(root block) with @keyframes offsetting stroke-dashoffset. TASTEFUL: thin (1-2px) pastel stroke living in the page margins, subtle, you feel it before you notice it (Huly-grade). SLOP: thick neon snake center-stage. This is the single mechanic that justifies 'the eternal return' rather than stating it.

findings.point: Magnetic CTA button — physics, not gimmick
findings.detail: On pointermove within a padded hit-area, translate the button toward the cursor by a fraction of the delta: target = (mouse - center) * strength (strength ~0.3-0.4); animate current -> target each frame with lerp: cur += (target-cur)*k, k~0.15 (reaches ~99% in ~50ms at 60fps, the tested sweet spot). On pointerleave, target=0 so it eases home. TASTEFUL: ONE magnetic element on the page (the primary 'Revive Your Dead Leads' CTA), small pull radius, label text lags slightly less than the shell for depth. SLOP: every button magnetic, huge pull radius, blobby morphing. Narrative fit: the CTA literally 'attracts' you — the pull toward rebirth. Mobile: no pointer, so it's inert (fine).

findings.point: Cursor-reactive field — the ambient generative hero (Huly-grade)
findings.detail: A canvas/WebGL field of faint particles or a flow-field ring segment that the cursor perturbs. Cheapest tasteful version: a low-opacity dot/glow that lerp-follows the cursor (k~0.14) and slightly warps nearby hero particles. Best generative version: particles seeded on the ouroboros path, drifting; near the cursor they scatter (death) then re-coalesce onto the path (rebirth) — a live metaphor. TASTEFUL: monochrome/pastel, low contrast, <60 particles, dies to a static gradient still-frame under reduced-motion. SLOP: rainbow trails, custom cursor that hides the real cursor, laggy heavy shaders that tank Core Web Vitals. Keep it to the hero only.

findings.point: Scroll-driven section reveals — use NATIVE CSS, author finished-state first
findings.detail: Golden rule: the finished (visible, in-place) state is your CSS default; the animation is layered only where supported so non-supporting browsers just show the final state. Use native animation-timeline: view() with animation-range to fade+rise sections (translateY 16px->0, opacity 0->1) as they enter — no JS, no observer, GPU-cheap. Gate the whole block in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference). TASTEFUL: 12-20px travel, ease-out, stagger children ~60ms, one reveal per section. SLOP: 100px slams, blur-in on everything, elements that reveal then keep jittering. caniuse ~82% global (2026) so the finished-state-default fallback is mandatory, not optional.

findings.point: Animated counters for the real proof numbers
findings.detail: Count 41%, 500,000+, 200,000+, 536% up from 0 when the stat row scrolls into view (IntersectionObserver, fire once). Ease-out so it decelerates into the true value: value = target * (1-(1-t)^3), t over ~1.1-1.6s; use requestAnimationFrame + Intl.NumberFormat for thousands separators; add font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums to stop width jitter. Pair the '8% industry vs 41% us' as two bars racing. TASTEFUL: count once, land exactly on the real figure, respect reduced-motion by rendering the final number immediately. SLOP: counting on every scroll pass, fake/rounded numbers, spinning odometers that overshoot. Narrative: numbers 'come back to life' as you arrive.

findings.point: Section 'composting' transitions = the death/rebirth beat
findings.detail: Between major sections, the outgoing section visually decays into the incoming one instead of a hard cut. Cheap tasteful implementation: as section A scrolls out, its content desaturates + drops opacity + a fine grain/dither mask dissolves it, while section B's first element inherits A's accent color then blooms into the new palette — 'ash feeds the next growth.' Tie it to the ouroboros: each section boundary is a point on the ring, so scroll progress along the serpent == progress through death->rebirth cycles. TASTEFUL: subtle mask/opacity crossfade over ~1 viewport of scroll, driven by ScrollTrigger scrub. SLOP: full-screen pinned scroll-jack that traps the user, long forced dwell, WebGL page-melt transitions. Give an easy escape (never fully pin > 1 screen).

findings.point: Interactive revenue calculator — the conversion centerpiece
findings.detail: Live-updating recoverable revenue = leads * pctNonBuyers * replyRate * convertRate * AOV. Sliders/inputs update result on every input event (no submit). TASTEFUL: result number animates by lerp toward the new target (k~0.2) so it feels alive but settles fast; a small ouroboros arc fills proportional to the recovered figure; prefill defaults to the real benchmark (41% reply) with a note it's editable; show the money figure huge, tabular-nums, GBP formatted. Add a 'this is money you already paid to acquire and wrote off' line = the death-rebirth thesis made personal. SLOP: laggy recompute, results that bounce/overshoot the real value, fake-inflated defaults, requiring email to see the number. Reduced-motion: number snaps instantly, no arc animation. This is where the ouroboros stops being decoration and becomes an argument.

findings.point: Reduced-motion is a first-class design target, not an afterthought
findings.detail: Author every effect so @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) yields a complete, premium static page: ring drawn to full (loop shown closed as a still), sections at final position/opacity, counters showing final numbers, calculator fully functional with instant results, magnetic/cursor effects disabled. Parallax and path-follow especially can trigger vestibular symptoms, so they must be fully gated. Never gate CONTENT behind motion. Also honor Save-Data / low-power by skipping the WebGL field. This protects Core Web Vitals (no layout shift, lazy assets) which 2026 sources flag as a make-or-break for premium pages.

deliverables: RECOMMENDED INTERACTION SET (exactly 6 + calculator): (1) Global scroll-drawn ouroboros ring stroke (spine). (2) One magnetic primary CTA. (3) Cursor-reactive generative particle field in hero only. (4) Native CSS view()-timeline section reveals. (5) One-shot easing counters on the real proof stats. (6) Composting section-boundary crossfades scrubbed to the ring. Plus the live revenue calculator. Nothing else animates.

OUROBOROS RING MATH: L = path.getTotalLength(); dasharray = L; dashoffset = L*(1 - p) where p = window.scrollY / (document.body.scrollHeight - innerHeight), clamped [0,1]. Serpent head geometry placed via path.getPointAtLength(L*p) to draw the biting-head glyph at the live tip. Native fallback: `@supports (animation-timeline: scroll()) { .ring{animation:draw linear;animation-timeline:scroll(root block)} @keyframes draw{from{stroke-dashoffset:var(--L)}to{stroke-dashoffset:0}} }`.

MAGNETIC BUTTON: target = {x:(mx-cx)*0.35, y:(my-cy)*0.35}; per-frame cur += (target-cur)*0.15; label lags at 0.22. Radius = button rect padded ~40px. Reset target=0 on pointerleave. Disable when (pointer:coarse) or reduced-motion.

LERP / EASING CONSTANTS: cursor field k=0.14; magnetic shell k=0.15, label k=0.22; calculator number k=0.2. Counter ease-out cubic value=target*(1-(1-t)^3) over 1.2s. Standard section ease: cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1), travel 16px, opacity 0->1, child stagger 60ms.

CALCULATOR FORMULA: recoverable = leads * pctNonBuyers * replyRate * convertRate * AOV. Sensible defaults grounded in real proof: replyRate=0.41, and label the output 'Revenue you already paid to acquire and wrote off — reborn.' Format with new Intl.NumberFormat('en-GB',{style:'currency',currency:'GBP',maximumFractionDigits:0}). Ouroboros arc fills = min(recoverable/target,1).

PASTEL PALETTE (ouroboros: pale ash -> living bloom): base paper #F6F4EF, ink #1C1A17, muted taupe/ash #B9B2A6, sage rebirth #A7C4A0, dusty rose decay #E7C7C2, faint gold serpent-stroke #C9B583, soft lilac accent #C9C2E0. Keep contrast low and monochrome-leaning; use ONE accent (sage=rebirth) for the CTA and calculator result. Dark variant: ink base #14130F, bone text #EDE8DF, same accents dimmed ~15%.

SVG STRATEGY: hand-author ONE continuous ouroboros path (open path so the head/tail gap is visible until p=1). Keep it as an inline <svg> fixed to the viewport at low z-index in the page margins, vector-effect:non-scaling-stroke, preserveAspectRatio to hug edges. Serpent head as a separate small group positioned via getPointAtLength. Provide a static 'closed loop' PNG/SVG poster for reduced-motion. Avoid rasterizing — the thin stroke must stay crisp at any DPR.

STACK: Next.js (App Router) + Lenis for damped smooth-scroll (feeds a single normalized progress value to everything) + GSAP ScrollTrigger with scrub for the ring + composting (pin max 1 screen) + Motion/Framer Motion for the React calculator and counters + native CSS animation-timeline:view() for section reveals (progressive enhancement). Canvas 2D (not heavy WebGL) for the hero particle field to protect Core Web Vitals. No scroll-jacking library; Lenis stays subtle.

REDUCED-MOTION CONTRACT: wrap ALL motion in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)`; the default CSS renders finished states. JS checks matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') before starting rAF loops, disables magnetic/cursor/particle field, snaps counters and calculator to final values, and shows the ring already fully drawn (loop closed). Also skip the particle field on (pointer:coarse) and when navigator.connection.saveData is true. Never hide content behind motion; zero layout shift.

designResults.topic: Lightweight stack + performance/accessibility architecture for OURO PRAXIS — a fast static site whose centerpiece is a custom canvas/WebGL generative ouroboros animation
designResults.summary: Recommendation: Vite + vanilla TypeScript + a single WebGL2 fragment-shader canvas, deployed to Vercel (GitHub-connected). For OURO PRAXIS the site is ~1 long marketing page whose one heavy interactive element is the generative ouroboros hero; that profile is the worst possible fit for Next static export (ships an 80-460KB React/RSC runtime you never use) and slightly over-engineered for Astro (islands buy you nothing when there is exactly one island and little static content to island-ify). Vanilla Vite gives the leanest baseline so your shader IS the payload, total control of the single requestAnimationFrame loop, and no framework tax fighting your 8ms-per-frame mobile budget. Keep the LCP element as server-rendered hero text (never the canvas), boot the animation after load via requestIdleCallback, cap DPR, offload to OffscreenCanvas in a worker where supported, and honor prefers-reduced-motion by painting one static ouroboros frame. Astro is the only defensible alternative and becomes the right call the moment the site grows to multiple content pages / MDX blog. Full concrete specs (palette, ouroboros parametric + shader strategy, CWV budgets, mobile tuning) in deliverables.

findings.point: Stack verdict: Vite + vanilla TS + WebGL2, not Next, not (yet) Astro
findings.detail: Next static export ships React runtime + router + RSC payload (~80-120KB baseline, benchmarks cite up to 463KB JS on comparable sites) on every page even with output:'export' — pure dead weight for a one-page marketing site with a single canvas. Astro ships 0KB JS by default and hydrates the canvas as one island, but islands are a content-site pattern; with exactly one interactive element and little prose to statically render, the island machinery adds a build/config layer with no payoff. Vanilla Vite gives the smallest possible baseline (your shader + ~2-4KB glue), instant HMR, tree-shaken ESM output, and zero framework contention for the main thread — decisive when your per-frame scripting budget on mid mobile is ~8ms.

findings.point: Host: Vercel over GitHub Pages
findings.detail: Both serve static output from the same GitHub repo. Vercel wins for this project: zero base-path config (GH Pages project sites force a /repo-name subpath that breaks absolute asset URLs and shader fetches unless you set Vite `base`), automatic Brotli + global CDN, per-PR preview deploys, and trivial custom-domain + fetchpriority-friendly headers. Use GH Pages only if the team mandates it — then set `base:'/repo/'` in vite.config and use relative asset URLs.
findings.source: vercel.com/i/astro-vs-next-js; Vite static-deploy docs (base config for GH Pages)

findings.point: The canvas must NOT be the LCP element
findings.detail: A <canvas> is a poor, late-painting LCP candidate and its first paint is gated on JS execution. Structure the hero so the LCP element is server-rendered heading/subhead text (present in the initial HTML), sitting ABOVE or OVER the canvas. The canvas then boots after load and fades in. This keeps LCP fast (text paints immediately) while the heavy animation stays off the critical path.

findings.point: Boot animation off the critical path; protect INP
findings.detail: A canvas rAF loop eats ~8-15ms scripting/frame and can tank INP on low-end devices. Defer WebGL init to after `load` via requestIdleCallback (fallback setTimeout 0). Break any heavy setup (shader compile, texture gen) into chunks yielded with scheduler.yield() / setTimeout so the main thread stays responsive to taps. Never run the loop when the hero is scrolled out of view — gate start/stop with IntersectionObserver so scripting drops to 0 below the fold.

findings.point: DPR handling: measure, cap, and scale — never hardcode
findings.detail: Read devicePixelRatio, but CAP it (2 on desktop, 1.5 on mobile) because fill-rate cost scales with pixel count squared — an uncapped DPR of 3-4 on phones is the single biggest 60fps killer for full-screen shaders. Set canvas.width = cssW * cappedDpr; canvas.height = cssH * cappedDpr; drive layout size via CSS (width:100%); pass resolution as a uniform to the shader (or ctx.scale(dpr,dpr) for 2D). Re-measure on ResizeObserver, not window resize, and debounce reallocation.

findings.point: OffscreenCanvas in a worker (with graceful fallback)
findings.detail: transferControlToOffscreen() moves the entire render loop off the main thread — the animation keeps 60fps even while the main thread handles scroll/taps, and INP is insulated from frame work. Supported in all modern browsers incl. iOS Safari 16.4+. Feature-detect: if typeof OffscreenCanvas === 'function' && canvas.transferControlToOffscreen, run in worker; else fall back to a main-thread loop with the identical render module (share the code). Post scroll-progress + reduced-motion state to the worker via postMessage.
findings.source: dev.to OffscreenCanvas-web-workers; MDN Optimizing_canvas (Safari 16.4 support note)

findings.point: prefers-reduced-motion: paint one static frame, don't just slow it
findings.detail: Combine the media query with a localStorage toggle using logical OR so either the OS setting OR an on-page control triggers reduced motion. In reduced mode, render a SINGLE static frame of the fully-formed ouroboros (freeze the rebirth phase at its most resolved state) instead of the loop — this is more accessible AND improves INP/CPU. Wire a visible 'reduce motion' toggle in the footer/nav. Also watchMedia the query so a mid-session OS change stops the loop live.
findings.source: blog.openreplay.com prefers-reduced-motion; thriveagency (disabling animation helps INP, not hurts)

findings.point: Below-the-fold: content-visibility + lazy media + loop gating
findings.detail: Apply content-visibility:auto (with contain-intrinsic-size to avoid CLS) to below-fold sections so the browser skips their layout/paint until near-viewport. Images: AVIF/WebP with explicit width/height, loading='lazy', and fetchpriority='high' ONLY on the single LCP asset. The canvas loop is itself lazy-loaded: IntersectionObserver stops rAF when hero exits, restarts on re-entry.

findings.point: 60fps on mid mobile: adaptive quality + fixed-timestep
findings.detail: Detect a low-power tier (navigator.hardwareConcurrency<=4 or matchMedia('(pointer:coarse)')) and downshift: lower DPR cap, fewer shader iterations / particles, optionally target 30fps by skipping alternate frames. Use a fixed-timestep accumulator with a delta clamp so motion is speed-stable across framerates and a background-tab spike can't produce a huge jump. Prefer GPU work (WebGL fragment shader) over per-pixel 2D canvas — the GPU keeps the main thread free. Pre-render truly static layers (grain, vignette, the fixed ouroboros ring) into cached offscreen buffers and only redraw the moving serpent/field.
findings.source: docs.bswen.com canvas-performance; svggenie.com svg-vs-canvas-vs-webgl-2026 (GPU offload); 7tonshark pixel-art-canvas-resize

findings.point: Scroll interactions: prefer native scroll-driven animations, JS-drive the shader uniform
findings.detail: For DOM reveal/parallax use CSS animation-timeline: scroll()/view() where supported (compositor-driven, no JS, no INP cost) with a small rAF fallback for Safari. For the hero itself, don't animate on scroll with layout — instead map scrollY→a normalized 0..1 'rebirth phase' uniform passed into the shader/worker, so the ouroboros visibly completes its death→rebirth cycle as the user scrolls. Avoid pulling in GSAP+ScrollTrigger (~50KB+) unless a storyboard demands pinning; a ~30-line IntersectionObserver + rAF progress mapper covers this brand's minimalist needs.

deliverables: STACK: Vite 5 + TypeScript, single-page. Deps kept minimal — no framework. Optional micro-libs only if needed: `regl` (~7KB) or `twgl.js` for terse WebGL2 setup, OR raw WebGL2 (0 deps). AVOID Three.js (~150KB+ gzip) — massive overkill for a single 2D-projected ouroboros field. Total target JS budget: <25KB gzip incl. shader glue. Deploy: Vercel, GitHub-connected, auto Brotli + CDN + preview deploys. vite.config: build.target='es2020', no base path needed on Vercel.

PROJECT SHAPE: index.html with server-present hero text (the LCP element) + <canvas id='ouro'> layered behind it (position:fixed/absolute, z-index below text). /src/main.ts = idle-boot + feature-detect OffscreenCanvas + IntersectionObserver gate + reduced-motion resolver. /src/render.worker.ts = the loop (imported by both worker and main-thread fallback). /src/ouro.glsl = fragment shader. Sections below fold get content-visibility:auto.

OUROBOROS GEOMETRY (parametric, for 2D-canvas variant or as shader SDF): base ring radius R; body centerline C(t)=(cx+R·cos t, cy+R·sin t), t∈[0,2π). Body half-thickness tapers head→tail: w(t)=w0·(0.15+0.85·(1−t/2π)) giving a fat head, thin tail tucked into the mouth. Add organic wobble: R(t)=R·(1+a·sin(k·t+φ(time))) with k=5..7 (superformula-lite lobes), a≈0.04 (subtle). 'Rebirth phase' p∈[0,1] (from scroll or time) drives φ(time)=2π·p and a dissolve mask: fragments with arc-position>p render as dispersing particles (death),

SHADER STRATEGY (preferred, GPU-cheap, Huly-grade): full-screen quad, single fragment shader. Distance-to-ring SDF d=abs(length(uv−center)−R)−w(angle). Soft body via smoothstep(w,w−fw,d). Overlay a domain-warped flow field (2-3 octaves of cheap value noise) tinted in the pastel palette to read as 'energy/soul' cycling around the ring — this is the generative math-driven motion. Uniforms: u_time, u_resolution, u_dpr, u_phase (scroll rebirth 0..1), u_reduced (freeze flag), u_quality (iteration count for mobile downshift). Iterations: noise octaves 3 desktop / 1-2 mobile. This keeps per-frame cost on the GPU; main thread only posts uniforms.

PASTEL PALETTE (light-first, premium/minimal): bg canvas-paper #F6F4EF; ink text #26242E; ouroboros gradient death→rebirth: ash #C7C2CE → blush #F3D3D8 → lilac #D8C6F0 → sage-mint #BFD8C8 → gold-leaf accent #E6C98B (the single warm 'value/cash' accent, used sparingly on the head/mouth). Soul-field glow #EAD9F5 at low opacity. Dark-mode variant: bg #17161B, ink #EDEBF2, same accents at slightly higher chroma. Keep contrast ≥4.5:1 for all text; the canvas is decorative (aria-hidden) so it needn't meet text contrast.

CWV BUDGETS (targets): LCP ≤2.0s (server-rendered hero text, fonts preloaded + font-display:swap, no canvas dependency); INP ≤150ms (loop in worker; init chunked with scheduler.yield; loop gated off below fold); CLS <0.05 (canvas + all media have explicit dimensions / contain-intrinsic-size). Per-frame scripting budget ≤4ms main-thread (ideally 0 when worker-offloaded), GPU frame ≤10ms. Lighthouse mobile target ≥95 perf, 100 a11y.

DPR + RESIZE SNIPPET (concept): const cap = matchMedia('(pointer:coarse)').matches?1.5:2; const dpr=Math.min(devicePixelRatio||1,cap); canvas.width=Math.round(cssW*dpr); canvas.height=Math.round(cssH*dpr); (2D: ctx.scale(dpr,dpr); WebGL: gl.viewport + u_resolution/u_dpr uniform). Drive off a debounced ResizeObserver; reuse buffers, reallocate only on real size change.

REDUCED-MOTION RESOLVER: const reduced = matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches || localStorage.getItem('ouro-reduce')==='1'; if(reduced) render one frame at u_phase=1 (fully reborn serpent) and DO NOT schedule rAF. Add matchMedia change listener to stop a running loop live. Visible footer toggle writes localStorage and re-resolves.

OFFSCREEN + FALLBACK GATE: if(window.OffscreenCanvas && canvas.transferControlToOffscreen){ const off=canvas.transferControlToOffscreen(); worker.postMessage({canvas:off,dpr,reduced,palette},[off]); } else { import('./render.js').then(m=>m.mainThreadLoop(canvas,...)); }  IntersectionObserver on hero posts {play:true/false} to worker (or starts/stops rAF). Scroll handler (passive, rAF-throttled) posts {phase: clamp(scrollY/heroHeight,0,1)}.

SCROLL/REVEAL: below-fold section reveals via CSS @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference){ .reveal{ animation: fade-up linear both; animation-timeline: view(); animation-range: entry 0% cover 30%; } } with a tiny IntersectionObserver+class fallback for Safari. No GSAP. Hero cycle is scroll-linked purely through the u_phase uniform — no layout animation, no jank.

marketingSynth.positioning: Ouro Praxis is the only premium, concept-led brand in the AI database-reactivation niche. Where every competitor runs the same GoHighLevel script behind loud, dollar-first, screenshot-wall landing pages, Ouro Praxis owns the trust-and-taste high ground the category has vacated. Positioning statement: "Ouro Praxis is the AI reactivation partner for local service businesses that refuse to accept a lead is ever truly dead. We revive the dormant leads already sitting in your CRM into booked calls and recovered revenue, on a recurring cycle, in your brand voice, without burning your list or your reputation. Not new acquisition. Transmutation of what you already own." The brand is built on the ouroboros, the serpent that eats its own tail: the eternal cycle of death and rebirth, where the end of a lead feeds the beginning of a sale. Strategic payload of the concept: it reframes reactivation from a one-off cash grab into a dignified, repeatable cycle, which is exactly what justifies recurring retainer pricing where competitors sell single blasts. Three moats competitors cannot copy on a 90-minute launch template: (1) compliance and sender-reputation stewardship as a visible pillar, (2) brand-voice fidelity so the AI sounds like the client and not a bot, (3) an Awwwards-caliber pastel-minimalist brand that signals premium before a word is read.
marketingSynth.offer: One flagship productized offer, "The Ouroboros Engine," a done-for-you reactivation cycle sold in three named phases. EXHUME: we audit, clean, dedupe, segment and compliance-scrub your dormant database to surface the buried buyers. REVIVE: a custom AI SMS agent trained on your brand voice reopens the conversation, answers questions, handles objections and qualifies intent in natural human-paced texting. RETURN: qualified prospects land on your calendar as booked appointments, the tail feeding the head. Praxis is the disciplined end-to-end execution of that cycle, run entirely for you. Value stack, each item anchored to a real cost the owner recognizes: full database exhumation and hygiene (the work of a data analyst); a custom-trained AI SMS agent in your brand voice, never a template (the output of a full-time SDR); conversation scripts engineered from campaigns that hit a 41% reply rate against an 8% industry average; done-for-you CRM and calendar integration, live in 7 days; a human handoff protocol so hot leads reach your team the moment they are ready to buy; weekly reactivation reporting on conversations, replies, booked calls and recovered revenue; optional add-on Engines for Speed-to-Lead (sub-60-second new-lead contact), Out-of-Hours booking, and Google Reviews auto-reply. Pricing architecture: a refundable activation fee (guide 2,000 to 3,000 GBP) plus a performance component (per booked appointment or a revenue share), never presented as a cost but always as a fraction of the recoverable revenue the client's own calculator surfaces. Honest scarcity, not fake timers: founder-built Engines cap monthly onboards, and one Engine per niche per market locks a competitor out for as long as the client stays. "You bring the list. We bring it back to life. One client per niche, per market."
marketingSynth.guarantee: The Rebirth Guarantee (recommended, a hybrid conditional plus performance structure that fully reverses buyer risk while protecting Ouro Praxis from bad inputs). Copy: "Your setup fee is 100% refundable if the Ouroboros Engine does not book at least [X] qualified appointments in your first 30 days. After that, you only ever pay for results we produce. We take the risk of reviving your list. You just take the calls." Conditions that keep it credible and filter tire-kickers, stated plainly: a minimum viable list size, the client must take the booked calls, and brand voice must be approved within 48 hours of onboarding. This is the strongest position on the Perceived-Likelihood axis of the value equation because it removes both entry risk (refundable activation) and ongoing risk (pay for results), while the conditions signal a real system rather than a gamble. Two alternates held in reserve for different prospects: a pure pay-on-results version for previously-burned skeptics ("a small activation fee, then only for qualified appointments we actually book"), and an ROI-multiple work-free version for confident high-AOV clients ("at least 3x your investment in recovered revenue within 90 days or we keep running the Engine free until you hit it").
narrativeSpine: THE FRAME (hero): The end of a lead is the beginning of a sale. Open on the ouroboros, the eternal cycle. Dead is a label the owner assigned, not a fact. Reframe the CRM from graveyard to cycle before making a single claim.

THE DORMANT (problem / nigredo, the blackening): Name the owner's real, guilt-laced reality. 71 to 73% of leads never worked, 47-hour average response times, thousands of contacts doing nothing. This is stored energy, not failure. Money already spent, sitting dead.

THE CYCLE (mechanism / how it works): The Ouroboros Engine in three alchemical phases, Exhume then Revive then Return, mapped to nigredo (the dead list), albedo (AI SMS revives and qualifies at dawn), rubedo (booked calls, the gold). The metaphor does the explaining.
THE FOUR SERPENTS (products): Four bots presented as four segments of one closing loop, each a coil of the same serpent. Reactivation, Speed-to-Lead, Out-of-Hours, Reviews.

THE RETURN (proof / metrics): The numbers a dead list produces once it is alive again. 41% vs 8% shown as a ratio, £500k+ recovered, 200k+ leads returned, 536% conversion lift, presented as a quiet premium data row plus animated counters, never a screenshot wall.
THE TRANSMUTATION ENGINE (calculator): Feed in base matter (list size, AOV), watch it resolve to gold (recoverable revenue). The number closes the loop as it settles.
THE VOICES (testimonials): Four verticalized owner voices, each carrying one earned proof point, each front-loading the death so the rebirth lands.

THE ETERNAL RETURN (offer + guarantee + objections): The Ouroboros Engine, the Rebirth Guarantee, and the trust pillar competitors lack (no burning your list). Recurring cycle framing justifies the retainer. Amor fati: want the return.
PRAXIS (final CTA): From philosophy to action. The cycle is the idea. Praxis is where it pays. Begin the cycle.
voiceGuide: Register: calm premium authority. The confident quiet of a brand that does not need to shout because the concept and the numbers do the work. Think a private bank or a heritage watchmaker that happens to run AI, not a conversion-bro funnel.

Theme discipline: death is always the doorway, never the dwelling. USE cycle, return, revive, renew, reawaken, dawn, gold, transmute, dormant, closed loop, second life, stored energy. HARD-AVOID corpse, grave, tomb, necromancy, zombie, skulls, goth or black-metal styling, and literal resurrection-of-the-dead imagery. One tasteful exception: the word graveyard may appear in an owner's own quoted voice or a single problem-section line, because owners genuinely say 'my CRM is a graveyard.' Never in headlines.
Reframe, do not gore. 'Dormant, not dead.' 'The conversation died, the lead never did.' Rebirth is carried by meaning, not morbidity.

Escape the category cliche. Never lead with a dollar sign or a '$10K in 48 hours' hype headline, that is exactly where every competitor lives. Lead with the concept and the reframe; let the numbers arrive as quiet proof.

Every claim traces to a real proof point: 41% reply vs 8% industry, £500k+ generated, 200k+ leads reactivated, 536% conversion lift, 10+ businesses, £40k to £100k recovered per campaign, <60s speed-to-lead. Invent nothing beyond these.
Sell the two things the owner actually wants: certainty and zero lift. 'You bring the list, we bring it back to life.' 'You just take the calls.' Reclaimed time as much as reclaimed money.

Mirror owner words in headlines and emotional copy (leads just sitting there, money you already spent, ghosted quotes, they went with whoever answered first). Use vendor words in functional labels (reactivation, reply rate, close rate, AOV, booked appointments).

Mechanics: NO em-dashes anywhere; use commas, periods, or parentheses. NO banned filler verbs (unlock, leverage, empower, supercharge, revolutionize, seamless, elevate, harness, unleash, delve). Prefer short declarative sentences. Contractions are fine and warm.

Naming: OURO (the cycle, ouroboros) plus PRAXIS (the doing). 'The cycle is the philosophy. Praxis is where it pays.' Capitalize product and phase names (The Ouroboros Engine, Exhume, Revive, Return) to make them read as proprietary IP.

Typographic restraint matches verbal restraint: sparse copy, generous whitespace, the pastel gold-thread ouroboros faint behind content. Let the counters and the calculator be the loudest thing on the page, and even they settle quietly rather than spin.

items.a: Protecting your sender reputation is our job, not your homework. We handle A2P registration, scrub against reassigned-number databases, honour every opt-out instantly, and keep STOP rates well under carrier limits with compliant messaging. We reactivate without ever burning your list, because your number reputation is the asset.

items.q: My leads are basically dead. Is there really anything left?

items.q: Do I need to hire anyone or change my systems?

items.q: How quickly do I see results?

Most clients recover £40,000 to £100,000 from a single campaign, and across clients this has driven a 536% increase in conversions and over £500,000 in generated revenue.

The most valuable asset a business owns is the list it has stopped calling, every dormant contact money already spent to earn attention that was never converted. palette._doctrine: Premium pastel = warm near-black INK on warm off-white PAPER, with desaturated/warmed pastels (S 25-42%, L 85-92%) used only as light, gradient washes and small chips — never large flat fills. Exactly one dark anchor (plum = the death/serpent pole) and one warm rebirth accent (clay/gold). Mandatory fine-grain overlay. This is the death->rebirth axis expressed as color: cold ash pastels resolve into warm living pastels as the rebirth pulse passes.
