hero_canvas.lines: dormant particle color @ alpha 0.15-0.4, drawn with globalCompositeOperation='multiply' (light bg deepens softly, never blows out)

typography.doctrine: Editorial light-weight serif display (cultural gravitas for the death/rebirth theme) + neutral grotesque body (clarity) + monospace for all numerals/eyebrows (reinforces the cycle/counter motif; makes the revenue calculator read as an instrument). Bold pastel headlines read cheap — keep display weights 300-400 with tight negative tracking.

fonts.display: Fraunces (variable) — opsz at max, wght 300-400, soft+wonky axes on, tracking -0.02em, line-height 0.95-1.02
fonts.mono: Geist Mono — numerals, eyebrows, labels, calculator; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums
fonts.loading: next/font (self-host, font-display:swap, preload display+body). Paid upgrade path: PP Editorial New (display) + PP Neue Montreal (body).
fonts.fallbacks: display: Georgia, serif · body/mono: system-ui / ui-monospace

scale_fluid.eyebrow: 0.8125rem (13px), UPPERCASE, letter-spacing 0.10em, Geist Mono, color inkSoft

rules.numerals: tabular-nums everywhere numbers change (counters, calculator) to kill width jitter
rules.eyebrowRationing: eyebrow used on AT MOST half the sections — never on hero, never two adjacent sections; it is a spice, not a default header

designSynth.stack:
DECISION: Next.js (App Router) + Tailwind v4 (@theme tokens holding the palette hex) + next/font (Fraunces + Geist + Geist Mono) + Lenis (damped smooth-scroll feeding ONE normalized 0..1 progress value to everything) + Framer Motion (React calculator + counters only) + native CSS scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline: view()) for section reveals + a SINGLE vanilla Canvas2D generative hero (no three.js, no WebGL) + inline SVG feTurbulence grain. Deploy: Vercel (GitHub-connected, auto Brotli + CDN + per-PR previews).

JUSTIFICATION / TRADEOFFS CONSIDERED:
- Canvas2D over WebGL/three.js: at the target 2,000-3,000 particles the proximity-line 'constellation' look is bottlenecked by line OVERDRAW, not particle count, and additive/multiply Canvas2D with shadowBlur is visually indistinguishable from a bloom shader at this scale — with ZERO dependencies (three.js is ~150KB+ gzip, pure waste here). Escalate to regl/three.js ONLY if a future shot needs true 3D torus-knot depth at 20k+ points.
- Next over vanilla-Vite (the leanest option): the site carries a stateful React revenue calculator, animated counters, and a magnetic-CTA/interaction layer that are materially cleaner in React, plus next/font solves the Fraunces variable-font loading cleanly. The vanilla-Vite+WebGL path is genuinely leaner (~25KB JS) and is the right call IF the calculator is dropped — it is not. We claw the weight back by: server-rendering the hero TEXT as the LCP element (never the canvas), lazy/idle-booting the canvas after load, and refusing GSAP (native scroll-driven CSS covers reveals).
- No GSAP ScrollTrigger (~50KB+): the brand is minimalist; a ~30-line IntersectionObserver + rAF progress mapper plus native animation-timeline covers every reveal and the ring scrub without the payload. Add GSAP only if a storyboard later demands hard pinning.
- Canvas is NEVER the LCP element and boots via requestIdleCallback; rAF gated by IntersectionObserver (stops below fold) + visibilitychange (stops on hidden tab); DPR capped at 2 desktop / 1.5 mobile; OffscreenCanvas+worker where supported with a shared main-thread fallback module.
CWV budget: LCP <=2.0s, INP <=150ms, CLS <0.05, per-frame main-thread <=4ms.
designSynth.heroAnimationSpec:
CONCEPT: a live, math-driven generative field on a warm pastel canvas — ~2,500 particles seeded along a large faint OUROBOROS RING, advected by divergence-free curl noise while spring-bound to the ring so the serpent silhouette holds but breathes, laced with proximity-lines (constellation shimmer), with a Gaussian REBIRTH PULSE travelling the loop from the mouth (dead->alive color shift) and cursor parallax. Light-bg equivalent of the Huly hero, but genuinely live (no baked video). Reduced-motion renders ONE resolved still frame.

LAYERS (draw order): L0 slow pastel mesh-gradient wash backdrop -> L1 faint ring guide (optional, alpha ~0.06) -> L2 particles -> L3 proximity lines (multiply) -> L4 rebirth pulse comet + persistent ring glint -> grain overlay (CSS, above all).

COORDINATES: cx,cy = canvas center. R = 0.38 * min(W,H). All physics use clamped dt = min((now-last)/1000, 1/30); speeds are per-second so framerate-independent.

1) RING BASE CURVE (superellipse, premium soft-square; mouth at t=0):
   x(t) = cx + A*sign(cos t)*|cos t|^(2/n)
   y(t) = cy + B*sign(sin t)*|sin t|^(2/n),   A=B=R, n in [2.5,4], t in [0,2π)
   Organic wobble: multiply radius by (1 + 0.06*sin(3t + 0.2*T)).
   Unit tangent Tang(t)=C'(t)/|C'(t)|; unit normal N(t)=(-Tang.y, Tang.x).
   (Alt for stronger 'mouth eats tail' read: Bernoulli lemniscate x=A cos t/(1+sin^2 t), y=A sin t cos t/(1+sin^2 t) — its self-crossing IS the bite. Ship superellipse for v1.)
   Precompute a 512-sample LUT of C(theta) so no per-particle trig per frame.

2) PARTICLE SEEDING (band around the ring): each particle i holds base phase theta_i ~ uniform[0,2π), radial offset rho_i ~ truncated Gaussian(0, sigma=0.06*R), depth d_i ~ [0,1], noise seed.
   Rest position: base_i = C(theta_i) + rho_i * N(theta_i).
   Count N = clamp(round(W*H/12000), 2000, 3000) desktop; <=700 mobile (else swap to static poster).

3) CURL-NOISE FLOW FIELD (divergence-free => particles swirl, never clump — the 'alive' core):
   fBm potential psi(x,y,t) = Σ_{o=0..2} 0.5^o * simplex3(x*f*2^o, y*f*2^o, t*0.08), f≈0.0035/px.
   2D curl (central diff, eps=1px): vx = (psi(x,y+eps)-psi(x,y-eps))/2; vy = -(psi(x+eps,y)-psi(x-eps,y))/2.

4) INTEGRATOR (semi-implicit Euler, ring-bound + flow), per frame:
   (a) theta_i += omega*dt         // omega ≈ 0.06 rad/s slow rotation
   (b) target_i = C(theta_i) + rho_i*N(theta_i)
   (c) F_spring = k*(target_i - p_i)          // k ≈ 6.0 (hug ring)
   (d) F_flow   = A_flow * curl(p_i, T)        // A_flow ≈ 22 px (smear into flow)
   (e) vel_i += dt*(F_spring + F_flow); vel_i *= 0.86; p_i += dt*vel_i
   Tune k/A_flow ratio for the 'liquid outline' look. Draw radius = r*(0.6+0.9*d_i); alpha = 0.35+0.65*d_i.

5) REBIRTH PULSE (the death->rebirth beat, from the mouth at theta=0):
   phi(t) = (2π * (T / T_loop)) mod 2π,  T_loop ≈ 7s.
   dtheta_i = min(|theta_i-phi|, 2π-|theta_i-phi|)
   g_i = exp(-(dtheta_i^2)/(2*w^2)),  w ≈ 0.30 rad (pulse width)
   Apply: size_i *= (1 + 1.8*g_i); alpha_i += 0.6*g_i; color_i = lerpHSL(dead #C9C3D6, alive [mint/peach/rose], g_i).
   Plus a persistent bright comet at P=C(phi) with 3-5 fading tail samples, core #E6C98B->#B5654A (~1 in 6 warm ember), additive, shadowBlur 8 — the serpent's soul circling clockwise forever.

6) PROXIMITY LINES (spatial hash, O(n·k)): grid cell = d_max=150px (or 28px tight look); bucket by floor(p/cell); test 3x3 neighbor cells only; cap 4 connections/particle.
   lineAlpha = 0.5 * pow(1 - dist/d_max, 2) * min(d_i,d_j).
   On the LIGHT bg use ctx.globalCompositeOperation='multiply' so overlapping pastel lines deepen softly (reserve 'lighter'/additive for the pulse/glint only).

7) CURSOR PARALLAX + SPOTLIGHT: normalize mouse to m in [-1,1]; smooth m_s += (m-m_s)*0.08.
   Depth parallax: p_draw_i = p_i + m_s * 18 * d_i (near particles move most).
   Pseudo-3D tilt: rotate field by m_s.x*0.05 rad; scale drawn y by cos(m_s.y*0.05).
   Spotlight reveal (Huly echo): within R=220px of cursor multiply node/line alpha by 1+1.2*(1-dist/R) and boost pulse spawn near cursor. Optional gentle attraction v += (cursor-node)*0.0006.

8) SCROLL COUPLING: map scrollY/heroHeight -> u_phase 0..1 passed as the pulse position bias so the ouroboros visibly 'completes' its cycle as you scroll (ties into the global ring spine).

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
- Single vanilla Canvas2D module (shared by OffscreenCanvas worker + main-thread fallback). Deps: 'simplex-noise' only.
- Boot after load via requestIdleCallback; DPR = min(devicePixelRatio, pointer:coarse?1.5:2); canvas.width=cssW*dpr, ctx.setTransform(dpr,0,0,dpr,0,0).
- Single rAF, dt-clamped. Pause via IntersectionObserver (hero off-screen) + visibilitychange.
- Fake bloom: shadowBlur 8-16 on additive pulse dots + one half-res offscreen pass blurred, drawn back with 'lighter' @ 0.4.
- Mobile: N<=700 or static poster.jpg + no field.
- REDUCED-MOTION / save-data / pointer:coarse: render exactly one frame at u_phase=1 (ring fully resolved, pulse at rest) and do NOT schedule rAF. matchMedia listener stops a running loop live. Canvas is aria-hidden decorative.
- LCP = server-rendered hero heading text OVER the canvas; canvas fades in after boot.
designSynth.logoConcept:
PRIMARY MARK — 'THE SINGLE BREATH' (hairline continuous-line ouroboros): a single near-circular open path whose ends overlap ~6deg (head just outside tail = the bite), a minimal 3-point head wedge, one filled eye-dot. Constant hairline stroke, fill:none, round caps/joins. Most premium/minimal; scales to favicon; pairs with the 'OURO PRAXIS' wordmark (Fraunces light, tracking +0.04em, small-caps 'PRAXIS').
  <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200'><path pathLength='1' d='…' fill='none' stroke='#3B2F4A' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/></svg>

ALTERNATE (large display / hero seal) — 'THE LIVING SERPENT' (organic filled taper): same geometry rendered as a FILLED body (SVG cannot taper a stroke), fat head -> hairline tail, true over/under bite, golden-position eye. Flat plum fill or a subtle death->life gradient #9AA0B0 -> #E9B7C4. Use sparingly.

SVG CONSTRUCTION (parametric generator, author once -> freeze static `d` string -> ship inline as a <symbol> that logo + watermark + reduced-motion poster all reference):
  Inputs: R (centerline), wTail≈1.5, wHead≈19.1, taperK≈1.3, spiralOffset≈wHead, headAngle, N=80-120 samples.
  Loop theta 0->2π:
    r(t) = R + spiralOffset*(t/2π)               // one-turn Archimedean spiral so head passes OUTSIDE tail (real over/under, no collision)
    w(t) = wTail + (wHead-wTail)*pow(t/2π, taperK)
    outer = (cx+(r+w)cos t, cy+(r+w)sin t);  inner = (cx+(r-w)cos t, cy+(r-w)sin t)
  Build d: walk OUTER edge t:0->2π (cubic-smoothed), round-cap tail at t=0, sculpt head wedge + mouth notch at t≈2π, walk INNER edge t:2π->0, 'Z'. Draw head last on top; add a paper-colored sliver in the overlap for over/under.
  GOLDEN-RATIO SHEET (phi=1.618, R=100): inner hole = R/phi = 61.8; body thickness 0.382R=38.2 (wHead=19.1); bite placed at golden angle 137.5deg from vertical; eye at 1/phi along head; mark:canvas ratio ~1/phi for breathing room.

MOTION (SVG+CSS only, no JS): self-draw via pathLength=1; stroke-dasharray:1; animate stroke-dashoffset 1->0 over 2.4s ease. Signature 'eternal return' = a second bright accent stroke (#E6C98B) with stroke-dasharray:'18 1000' looping stroke-dashoffset 0 -> -1018 (6s linear infinite) — a glint of life-force circling forever. ALL motion guarded by @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){ animation:none; stroke-dashoffset:0 }.
designSynth.backgroundMotif:
GIANT SUBTLE WATERMARK: reuse the SAME ouroboros <symbol> (single source of truth) via <use>, scaled to 120-160vmin, bleeding off one edge, opacity 0.035-0.05, color = plum ink, pointer-events:none, z-index:-1, filter:blur(0.4px) to soften thin-arc aliasing. Feather into the paper with a radial-gradient mask so edges dissolve (premium, not stamped). Ultra-slow rotation @keyframes spin 180s linear infinite; optional light scroll parallax. Because it is vector it stays crisp at any DPR.
  .wm{position:fixed;inset:auto -20% -30% auto;width:160vmin;opacity:.045;color:#3B2F4A;pointer-events:none;z-index:-1;filter:blur(.4px);-webkit-mask:radial-gradient(closest-side,#000 55%,transparent 100%);mask:radial-gradient(closest-side,#000 55%,transparent 100%);animation:spin 180s linear infinite}
  Upgrade: use the ouroboros path as a <mask> to cut it OUT of a soft pastel gradient blob (Huly-grade negative-space read).

GLOBAL SCROLL-SPINE (second motif, distinct from the watermark): one thin (1-2px) pastel/gold ouroboros ring living in the PAGE MARGINS, drawn by scroll progress via stroke-dashoffset = L*(1 - p) — unwritten at top, head meets tail at the final CTA (loop closes = dead leads reborn as revenue). vector-effect:non-scaling-stroke, fixed to viewport. Reduced-motion shows it already closed.

GRAIN OVERLAY (mandatory cheapness-killer, both motifs sit under it): fixed full-viewport div, inline SVG feTurbulence (baseFrequency 0.8, numOctaves 4) as a data-URI, opacity 0.04, mix-blend-mode:soft-light, pointer-events:none. Zero requests. This single layer is what makes the pastels read expensive rather than templated.
interactions: SPINE — Global scroll-drawn ouroboros ring (the narrative through-line): L=path.getTotalLength(); dasharray=L; dashoffset=L*(1-p), p=clamp(scrollY/(scrollHeight-innerHeight),0,1). Serpent head glyph placed at path.getPointAtLength(L*p). Native fallback: @supports(animation-timeline:scroll()){ .ring{animation:draw linear;animation-timeline:scroll(root block)} }. REDUCED-MOTION: ring rendered already closed (loop shown as a still). Thin margin stroke, felt-before-noticed — not a neon center snake.

HERO — Cursor-reactive generative particle field (see heroAnimationSpec): parallax + spotlight + attraction near cursor; particles scatter (death) and re-coalesce onto the ring (rebirth). REDUCED-MOTION / pointer:coarse / save-data: single resolved still frame, no rAF.

ONE Magnetic primary CTA ('Revive Your Dead Leads'): target={ (mx-cx)*0.35, (my-cy)*0.35 }; per-frame cur += (target-cur)*0.15; label lags at 0.22 for depth; pull radius = rect padded ~40px; reset target=0 on pointerleave. Exactly ONE magnetic element on the page. DISABLED when (pointer:coarse) or reduced-motion (static button, fully clickable).

Native CSS section reveals: @media(prefers-reduced-motion:no-preference){ .reveal{ animation:fade-up linear both; animation-timeline:view(); animation-range: entry 0% cover 30% } } — translateY 16-24px, opacity 0->1, child stagger 60-80ms, ease cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1). Tiny IntersectionObserver+class fallback for Safari. FINISHED STATE IS THE CSS DEFAULT — non-supporting browsers and reduced-motion just show the final in-place layout (never gate content behind motion).

One-shot easing COUNTERS on the real proof numbers (41% reply, 500,000+ GBP, 200,000+ leads, 536% lift): fire once on enter (IntersectionObserver), value=target*(1-(1-t)^3) over ~1.2s, Intl.NumberFormat + tabular-nums; pair '8% industry vs 41% us' as two racing bars. REDUCED-MOTION: render final numbers immediately. Never invent/round the real figures.

Composting section-boundary crossfades (the death/rebirth beat): outgoing section desaturates + drops opacity + a grain/dither mask dissolves it while the incoming section's first element inherits the accent then blooms — 'ash feeds the next growth'. Scrubbed to the ring spine (Lenis progress). NEVER pin >1 screen (no scroll-jack trap). REDUCED-MOTION: plain instant section swap.

CENTERPIECE — Live revenue calculator (the ouroboros becomes an argument): recoverable = leads * pctNonBuyers * replyRate * convertRate * AOV; updates on every input event (no submit, no email gate). Result number lerps toward target (k=0.2), huge, tabular-nums, GBP via Intl.NumberFormat('en-GB',{style:'currency',currency:'GBP',maximumFractionDigits:0}); a small ouroboros arc fills = min(recoverable/target,1). Default replyRate=0.41 (editable, labelled as the real benchmark). Framing line: 'Revenue you already paid to acquire and wrote off — reborn.' REDUCED-MOTION: number snaps instantly, no arc animation, fully functional.

CONTRACT: exactly these 6 + calculator animate — nothing else. Every effect is layered on a complete, premium static state; matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce)') is checked before any rAF loop; canvas field also skipped on pointer:coarse and navigator.connection.saveData. Zero layout shift; a visible 'reduce motion' toggle in the footer writes localStorage and re-resolves live.
sectionLayouts: FAMILY A — CENTERED HERO (full-bleed): generative ouroboros canvas fixed behind; server-rendered centered Fraunces display headline ('Convert Your Old Leads Into Cash On Demand') = the LCP element, one mono subhead line, single magnetic CTA. ~88-90vh, heavy top air. NO eyebrow here (rationing rule). The ring spine begins unwritten at the top margin.

FAMILY B — HORIZONTAL PROOF BAND: full-width tinted strip (paperSunken), a row of 3-4 mono tabular-num COUNTERS (41% reply · 500,000+ GBP generated · 200,000+ leads reactivated · 536% conversion lift) with the '8% industry vs 41% us' racing-bars pair beneath. Eyebrow OK here ('THE NUMBERS'). Distinct from A/C: it is a compressed data ribbon, not a content section.

FAMILY C — BENTO PRODUCT GRID (asymmetric, NOT zigzag): the 4 products (Database Reactivation Bot / Speed-to-Lead <60s / Out-of-Hours Bot / Google Reviews Bot) in an uneven bento — one tall feature cell + three supporting cells, 20-24px radii, hairline borders (no gray boxes), one pastel wash per card (lavender/mint/blush/sky), plum text-tints. Eyebrow ('WHAT WE BUILD'). This is the grid family — used once.

FAMILY D — SPLIT INSTRUMENT (the revenue calculator): asymmetric two-column — left = control panel (paperRaised card, sliders/inputs, mono labels), right = a giant tabular-num GBP result with the ouroboros arc filling around it and the 'money you wrote off, reborn' line. Reads as a measuring instrument, not a form. No eyebrow (the huge number is the header). The conversion centerpiece.

FAMILY E — EDITORIAL NARRATIVE COLUMN (the death->rebirth 'how it works' + founder Everett): single narrow ~680px prose column, generous leading, Fraunces subheads, steps aligned to points on the ring spine so scroll progress = progress through the cycle. Left-heavy editorial rhythm. Eyebrow allowed on ONE of the two (not both). This is the reading family — calm, text-first.

FAMILY F — FULL-BLEED ANCHOR CTA (loop closes): the ONE dark/plum section (uses the dark palette variant), centered, where the global ring spine reaches p=1 and the serpent bites its tail. Repeat of the magnetic CTA ('Ready to Revive Your Dead Leads?'), gold glint accent. High-contrast payoff moment. No eyebrow — this is the climax, it just says the thing.
FOOTER: minimal, ink-on-paper, hairline top border, mini ouroboros mark, nav, and the visible 'reduce motion' toggle (writes localStorage). Contact / Everett.

COMPOSITION DISCIPLINE: 6 distinct layout families (A-F) so no two adjacent sections share a shape and the classic left-text/right-image ZIGZAG is used at most once (inside E) — never as the site's default rhythm. EYEBROW RATIONING: eyebrows appear on at most B, C, and one of E — never on A, D, F, and never on two adjacent sections. Section vertical padding clamp(6rem,12vw,12rem); content max-width 1200-1280px; whitespace is the primary premium signal, ahead of color.
